tasks.description: Extract drop indicator management from useTimelineEditing.ts into new src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useDropIndicator.ts. Move ensureDropIndicator, clearDropIndicator, dropIndicatorRef, dragPreviewFrameRef, latestDragPreviewRef (lines ~114-204). Move root element inline styles (lines 162-168: position:fixed, inset:0, pointer-events:none, z-index:99999) to a .drop-indicator-root CSS class in timeline-overrides.css. Keep only dynamic positional styles (left, top, width, height) inline. Return { ensureDropIndicator, clearDropIndicator, latestDragPreviewRef, dragPreviewFrameRef }. Also include the cleanup useEffect (lines 234-238).

Extract drop position calculation from useTimelineEditing.ts into new src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useDropPosition.ts. Move getDropPosition (lines 206-232) and getTimelineDomNodes (lines 138-153) with timelineDomCacheRef (lines 105-113). Parameters: { dataRef, scale, scaleWidth }. Return { getDropPosition }.

Extract inferDragKind utility from useTimelineEditing.ts (lines 537-555) into src/tools/video-editor/lib/coordinate-utils.ts as a pure exported function. It only reads event.dataTransfer.types and event.dataTransfer.items plus calls getDragType. Remove the useCallback wrapper.

Extract external drag-drop handlers into new src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts. Move onTimelineDragOver, onTimelineDragLeave, onTimelineDrop, registerGenerationAsset, uploadImageGeneration. This hook consumes useDropIndicator (T1) and useDropPosition (T2), and inferDragKind (T3). Return { onTimelineDragOver, onTimelineDragLeave, onTimelineDrop, registerGenerationAsset }.

Extract library callbacks into new src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useLibraryCallbacks.ts. Move onActionMoveStart, onActionMoving, onActionMoveEnd, onActionResizeStart, onActionResizeEnd, onChange. Add JSDoc on each callback documenting the return-value contract: onActionMoving returns false to reject (crossTrackActive), undefined to accept; onChange returns false to reject (crossTrackActive), undefined to accept. Include clearActionDragState in this hook as it's consumed by both library callbacks and passed through to useClipDrag.

Slim down useTimelineEditing.ts to an orchestrator composing T1-T5 hooks. Should only contain clip mutation handlers: handleDeleteClip, handleSelectedClipChange, handleResetClipPosition, handleSplitSelectedClip, handleToggleMute, handleAddText, handleAssetDrop, onOverlayChange. Target ~250 lines. UseTimelineEditingResult interface and UseTimelineEditingArgs stay here. Run npm run build to validate.

Replace shared mutable actionDragStateRef with ActionDragCoordinator. Create src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActionDragState.ts with interface ActionDragCoordinator { startDrag, updatePosition, getLatest, getInitial, clear } — raw ref is internal only. Migrate the full 5-file chain: (1) useTimelineData.ts: replace ref declaration with useActionDragState(), update UseTimelineDataResult type. (2) useTimelineState.ts: replace actionDragStateRef with actionDragCoordinator in TimelineEditorContextValue (line 25), useTimelineEditing args (line 112), context object (line 149). (3) TimelineEditor.tsx: destructure actionDragCoordinator from context (lines 26,64), pass to useClipDrag. (4) useLibraryCallbacks.ts (from T5): replace direct ref mutations with coordinator methods. (5) useClipDrag.ts: update UseCrossTrackDragOptions type (line 29), replace reads (lines 304,331,357) and writes (line 434) with coordinator methods. (6) useTimelineEditing.ts orchestrator: update UseTimelineEditingArgs. Guard clear() against stale updates. Build-check after EACH file. Grep for actionDragStateRef — must be zero matches.

Document ClipAction stopPropagation coupling. Add comments to all 3 stopPropagation calls in ClipAction.tsx (lines 87, 89, 93). onPointerDown: 'Load-bearing: useClipDrag capture-phase pointerdown fires first but only takes pointer capture after move threshold (useClipDrag.ts:318-322). Without this, bubbled event reaches timeline library drag init, starting a competing drag.' onClick: 'Prevents timeline background click handler from deselecting the clip.' onDoubleClick: 'Prevents timeline background click handler from deselecting the clip.' No behavioral change — documentation only.

Fix EditorPaneTab generation pane height hack. Extend getEffectivePaneHeights in PanesContext.tsx (line 87) to accept isGenerationsPaneVisible: boolean. When false, set effectiveGenerationsPaneHeight to 0 and give editor full ideal height (capped by viewport). When true, use existing compression logic. Update PanesProvider call site (line 166) to pass generationsVisible (already computed at line 143 as locks.gens || isGenerationsPaneOpenState). Remove the hack in EditorPaneTab.tsx lines 41-43 — just use effectiveEditorPaneHeight directly. Update PanesContext.test.tsx with visible vs hidden generations pane cases. MIN_EDITOR_HEIGHT (200px, line 85) is the correct floor for video editor too.

Final validation: run npm run build, run npm test -- --watchAll=false, grep for actionDragStateRef in src/tools/video-editor/ (zero matches expected), verify useTimelineEditing.ts line count is ~250. Summarize results.

watch_items: Library callback return-value contracts MUST be preserved exactly: onActionMoving and onChange return false to reject, undefined to accept. Breaking this silently breaks move/resize.

Drop indicator root element: only move STATIC styles to CSS (.drop-indicator-root). Dynamic positional styles (left, top, width, height on child elements) must remain inline — they're set per-frame during drag.

actionDragStateRef threading chain spans 5 files (useTimelineData → useTimelineState → TimelineEditorContext → TimelineEditor → useClipDrag). All must be migrated in T7 — partial migration will break the build.

ClipAction onPointerDown stopPropagation is load-bearing (flag-clipaction-pointerdown-assumption verified). Do NOT remove it. useClipDrag only takes pointer capture after a move threshold; without stopPropagation the timeline library starts a competing drag.

flag-drop-indicator-shared-abstraction (minor, open): useClipDrag has its own drop indicator creation code. T1 extracts only the external drag path's indicators. This is intentional — don't try to unify them in this cleanup.
Step 7 is highest-risk: build-check after EACH file update in the 5-file migration, not after batching all 5.
EditorPaneTab height hack (T9): the generationsVisible flag already exists at PanesContext.tsx:143. Use it directly — don't introduce new state.
useTimelineEditing exports UseTimelineEditingResult interface consumed by useTimelineState.ts (ReturnType<typeof useTimelineEditing>). The orchestrator must preserve the exact same return type.
coordinate-utils.ts already exists with exports (buildRowTrackPatches, getCompatibleTrackId, etc.). inferDragKind goes there as an addition, not a new file.
The ActionDragCoordinator.clear() must guard against stale updatePosition calls arriving after clear — set an internal isCleared flag that causes late updates to no-op.

sense_checks.question: Does the new .drop-indicator-root CSS class in timeline-overrides.css contain exactly: position:fixed, top:0, left:0, width:100%, height:100%, pointer-events:none, z-index:99999? Are zero inline styles remaining on the root element creation?

Does useDropPosition.ts correctly import ROW_HEIGHT and TIMELINE_START_LEFT from coordinate-utils, and rawRowIndexFromY? Does getDropPosition handle the scrollLeft/scrollTop from the cached grid element?
Is inferDragKind exported as a plain function (not wrapped in useCallback) from coordinate-utils.ts? Does it still import getDragType from shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop?
Does useExternalDrop consume useDropIndicator and useDropPosition (not re-implement them)? Does the drag preview rAF loop still use latestDragPreviewRef from useDropIndicator?

Does every library callback that returns boolean|undefined have a JSDoc comment documenting the contract? Does onActionMoving return false when crossTrackActive.current is true, and undefined otherwise?

Is useTimelineEditing.ts now ~250 lines or less? Does it compose useDropIndicator, useDropPosition, useExternalDrop, and useLibraryCallbacks? Does UseTimelineEditingResult still export the exact same interface shape?
Does grep for 'actionDragStateRef' in src/tools/video-editor/ return zero matches? Does the ActionDragCoordinator interface NOT expose a ref property? Does clear() guard against stale updates?
Are all 3 stopPropagation calls retained in ClipAction.tsx with explaining comments? Does the onPointerDown comment reference the specific move-threshold lines in useClipDrag?

Does EditorPaneTab.tsx no longer contain any local height subtraction (the Math.max(200, effectiveEditorPaneHeight - generationsOffset) hack)? Does getEffectivePaneHeights accept isGenerationsPaneVisible and return effectiveGenerationsPaneHeight=0 when false? Are PanesContext tests updated?
Do npm run build AND npm test both pass? Is useTimelineEditing.ts line count confirmed at ~250? Is actionDragStateRef grep count confirmed at 0?

meta_commentary:
**Execution guidance:**

**Parallelism:** T1, T2, T3, and T5 have no dependencies — execute them in parallel. T4 blocks on T1+T2+T3. T6 blocks on T4+T5. T7, T8, T9 all block on T6 but are independent of each other — execute in parallel. T10 is the final gate.

**Highest-risk step is T7** (ActionDragCoordinator migration across 5 files). The plan says build-check after each file — this is critical because a type error in one file will cascade. Recommended order within T7: (1) Create useActionDragState.ts with the coordinator hook, (2) Update useTimelineData.ts to use it, (3) Update useTimelineState.ts types, (4) Update useLibraryCallbacks.ts (new from T5), (5) Update useClipDrag.ts, (6) Update TimelineEditor.tsx, (7) Update useTimelineEditing.ts orchestrator. Build after each.

**T9 has a subtle interaction:** getEffectivePaneHeights is currently called unconditionally at PanesContext.tsx:166. The generationsVisible variable already exists at line 143. Just add it as a parameter. When isGenerationsPaneVisible=false, short-circuit: return { effectiveEditorPaneHeight: Math.min(idealEditorPaneHeight, viewportHeight), effectiveGenerationsPaneHeight: 0 }. The existing compression logic only runs when visible.

**ActionDragState type re-export:** useClipDrag.ts (line 9) defines its own `ActionDragState` interface identical to useTimelineData.ts's. After T7, both should import from useActionDragState.ts. Check that the duplicate interface in useClipDrag.ts is removed.

**CSS class naming:** timeline-overrides.css already uses the `.drop-indicator-*` naming convention. The new `.drop-indicator-root` class fits naturally.

**Don't over-extract:** The plan intentionally does NOT unify useClipDrag's internal drop indicators with useDropIndicator (flag-drop-indicator-shared-abstraction is acknowledged as minor/open). Don't try to do this — it would change behavior in the internal pointer-based drag system.
